name: Malawi National Statistical Office
description: The NSO is responsible for collecting, compiling, analyzing, and disseminating official statistics. Their household surveys — including the Integrated Household Survey (IHS) and Demographic and Health Survey (DHS) — include audio recordings of interviews conducted in local languages across all districts.

name: The Times Group
description: Malawi's largest private media company, publishing The Daily Times and Malawi News. They produce news articles, opinion pieces, and investigative reporting in both English and Chichewa, offering a rich written corpus of contemporary Malawian language.

name: The Nation Publications
description: Publishers of The Nation and Weekend Nation newspapers. A major source of Chichewa and English news, feature writing, and editorial content covering politics, economics, culture, and society in Malawi.

name: Radio Islam
description: One of Malawi's most popular private radio stations, broadcasting in Chichewa and Yao. Programming includes news, religious content, community affairs, call-in shows, and cultural programs, providing extensive spoken-language data.

name: Transworld Radio
description: An international Christian broadcaster with a significant Malawian presence. Produces programming in Chichewa, Tumbuka, Yao, and Lomwe including educational content, health programming, storytelling, and agricultural extension shows.

name: Malawi Broadcasting Corporation
description: Malawi's national public broadcaster operating MBC Radio 1 and MBC Radio 2 (now known as Namikango FM). As the state broadcaster, MBC produces content in all major Malawian languages including news bulletins, parliamentary coverage, drama, and educational programming.

name: YONECO FM
description: Youth Net and Counselling (YONECO) operates a community radio station focusing on youth empowerment, gender issues, and human rights. Broadcasts primarily in Chichewa and Yao from Zomba district.

name: Nyanthepa Community Radio
description: A community radio station serving Mzimba and surrounding areas. Broadcasts primarily in Tumbuka with programming on agriculture, health, education, and local governance, providing valuable Tumbuka-language audio data.

name: Malawi Institute of Journalism
description: Malawi's premier journalism training institution. MIJ produces student and faculty media in multiple Malawian languages, including practice broadcasts, written news assignments, and multimedia projects.

name: Malawi Department of Information
description: The government's official information arm, producing press releases, public service announcements, policy documents, and informational content in Chichewa and English for nationwide distribution.

name: Malawi Institute of Education
description: Responsible for curriculum development and educational materials. MIE produces textbooks, teaching guides, and supplementary reading materials in Chichewa, Tumbuka, Yao, and other languages used in primary education.

name: Montfort Media
description: A Catholic media organization producing print, radio, and multimedia content in Chichewa and other local languages. Their output includes books, pamphlets, audio dramas, educational materials, and community development content.
